title: Recall sum-to-product formula for \sin A + \sin B
text: The sum-to-product formula for \sin A + \sin B is $$\sin$$ $$A$$ + $$\sin$$ $$B$$ $$=$$ 2\sin\left(\frac{A+B}{2}\right)\cos\left(\frac{A-B}{2}\right)$$.

title: Apply the sum-to-product formula
text: Substitute A and B into the formula to rewrite $$\sin(x)+\sin(3x)$$.

title: Further simplify using identities
text: Recall the double angle formula for sine $$(\$\$\sin(2x)=2\sin$$ $$x$$ \cos x$$) and the even identity for cosine $$(\$\$\cos(-x)=\cos$$ x$$).
